title: Belaf Release Manifest
description: Wire format produced by the belaf CLI and consumed by the belaf GitHub App. Version 2.0. Belaf is the schema-owner; downstream consumers vendor a copy of this file.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. The producer (CLI) emits the version it wrote; the consumer (App) dispatches to the matching parser. Bump only on breaking changes.

manifest_id.description: UUID v7 identifying this manifest. Sortable. The on-disk filename is `belaf/releases/<manifest_id>.json`.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when this manifest was generated. (No `format: date-time` keyword: belaf uses the `time` crate, not `chrono`. Validation happens in producer code.)

created_by.description: Username (typically GitHub login) of the person or service that triggered the release.

base_branch.description: Git branch the release will target on merge.

groups.description: Logical groupings of releases that must ship together with synchronised versions. Each group's members refer to entries in `releases[]` by name. Groups are optional; a manifest with no groups is just a flat release list.

releases.description: Per-project release entries. The CLI populates one entry per ecosystem-specific manifest file that needs a version bump. Members of a group share `group_id`.

x.description: Vendor-extension namespace. Strict: must be an object; content is unstructured and explicitly unsupported. Use to ship experimental fields ahead of a schema bump.

id.description: Group identifier. Lower-case ASCII, digits, and hyphens only; must start with a letter or digit. Max 64 characters. Used as `release.group_id` and may appear in `tag_format` templates.

members.description: Names of the releases that belong to this group. Each must equal a `release.name` value present in `releases[]`. All members of one group share the same bump and resulting version.

name.description: Project name as known to the ecosystem (e.g. `@org/foo` for npm, `com.org:foo` for Maven, `cargo-crate-name` for Rust).

ecosystem.description: Ecosystem identifier. Stored as a free-form string in the wire format; consumers classify against a hand-maintained whitelist (`KNOWN_ECOSYSTEMS`) into Known/Unknown variants. Adding a new ecosystem does not require a schema bump.

group_id.description: Optional foreign key into `groups[].id`. When set, this release is one member of a synchronised release group.

previous_version.description: Version this project shipped before the bump. Empty string for first-time releases.

bump_type.description: Stored as a free-form string; consumers classify against a hand-maintained whitelist (Known/Unknown). Standard values: `major`, `minor`, `patch`, `prerelease`.

tag_name.description: Git tag name to be created on release. The CLI is responsible for generating this and validating it against `git check-ref-format --allow-onelevel`. No default generator on the consumer side.

previous_tag.description: Git tag of the previous release, if one exists in the repository. Used by consumers to render a compare URL.

compare_url.description: Pre-built compare URL between `previous_tag` and `tag_name`, or null if this is a first-time release.

changelog.description: Markdown changelog body for this release. May be empty for first-time or no-content releases.

contributors.description: GitHub usernames of contributors whose commits are included in this release.

first_time_contributors.description: Subset of `contributors` who are appearing in a release for the first time.

statistics.description: Optional release statistics block. Stability suffix `_v1alpha1` may be added in future to subsystems within statistics that aren't yet stable.

commit_count.description: Number of commits between `previous_tag` and the release commit.

days_since_last_release.description: Days between `previous_tag` and `created_at`. Null for first-time releases.
